strengths: React: The candidate has used React in a project involving UI development and integration with a backend service, aligning with the listed market demand.
AWS: Experience with DynamoDB, S3, ECS, and Cognito demonstrates practical cloud skills, which are frequently preferred.
REST: The candidate has built and deployed a RESTful microservice, matching the demand in 25% of postings.

gaps.action: Complete a hands-on SQL tutorial on platforms like Khan Academy or Codecademy, focusing on basic queries and data manipulation. Aim for 10-15 hours of practice.

gaps.skill: Software Engineering Fundamentals (OOP, Data Structures, Algorithms)
gaps.action: Enroll in a Coursera or edX course on data structures and algorithms using Java or Python and complete practice problems on LeetCode (easy/medium level). Dedicate 2-3 hours per week for 3-4 months.

Complete the 'Develop in Swift Fundamentals' course available on the Apple Developer website or an equivalent Kotlin course in Android development. Aim to develop a basic mobile application (e.g., a simple to-do list app) following tutorials. Budget 3-4 hours per week for 3-4 months.

gaps.skill: Bachelor's Degree (Computer Science or related field)
gaps.action: Research part-time or online Bachelor of Computer Science programs at local universities like Toronto Metropolitan University or York University. Explore options to transfer credits from the Seneca Polytechnic diploma.

uniqueValue: Experience with both JavaFX application development and modern cloud-based microservices showcases versatility.
Full CRUD implementation experience signals a good understanding of data management principles.
Experience with CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions indicates familiarity with DevOps practices.
